description: Unix filename pattern matching using shell-style wildcards.

functions.name: fnmatch

params.name: pattern
params.description: The shell-style wildcard pattern to match against. Supports *, ?, [seq], and [!seq].

returns.description: True if filename matches the pattern, False otherwise.

functions.description: Test whether the filename matches the pattern. The comparison is case-insensitive on platforms where the file system is case-insensitive (e.g., Windows). Both filename and pattern are first case-normalized using os.path.normcase().

functions.name: fnmatchcase

params.name: pattern
params.description: The shell-style wildcard pattern to match against.

returns.description: True if filename matches the pattern, False otherwise.

functions.description: Test whether the filename matches the pattern with a case-sensitive comparison, regardless of the platform's file system case sensitivity.

functions.name: filter

params.name: pattern
params.description: The shell-style wildcard pattern to match against.

returns.description: A subset of names matching the pattern.

functions.description: Return the subset of the list of names that match the pattern. Equivalent to [n for n in names if fnmatch(n, pattern)] but implemented more efficiently.

functions.name: translate

params.name: pattern
params.description: The shell-style wildcard pattern to translate.

returns.description: A regular expression string equivalent to the shell pattern.

functions.description: Translate a shell wildcard pattern to a regular expression. The resulting regex can be used with re.match(). There is no way to quote metacharacters.
